title: pittosporum pervillei subsp. coursii
intro: Pittosporum pervillei subsp. coursii, also known as the 'Houhere' or 'New Zealand Pittosporum', is a plant species native to the forests of New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Pittosporum pervillei subsp. coursii lie in its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as its expectorant properties, which aid in relieving respiratory congestion. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum pervillei subsp. coursii can be found in the form of essential oils, which can be used in aromatherapy, and infused teas, which can be used to make topical applications for skin and respiratory issues.
